summary: This excerpt from Jürgen Schmidhuber's homepage outlines his lifelong ambition to build a self-improving AI smarter than himself so he can retire. It highlights his lab's foundational contributions to deep learning, such as LSTMs, and introduces 'Schmidhuber's law,' which posits that technological breakthroughs are accelerating exponentially toward an Omega point around 2040.

principles.statement: The ultimate goal of AI research is to build a self-improving Artificial Intelligence smarter than oneself, and then retire.
principles.rationale: Once an AI is capable of self-improvement and surpasses human intelligence, human effort in the field becomes obsolete.
principles.source_quote: Since age 15 or so, his main goal has been to build a self-improving Artificial Intelligence (AI) smarter than himself, then retire.

mental_models.name: Schmidhuber's Law (Computer history speedup)
mental_models.description: A model of technological acceleration where the time interval between major historical and computational breakthroughs halves with each successive milestone, converging toward a singularity.
mental_models.example: Using the historical speedup of breakthroughs to predict an 'Omega point around 2040'.

signature_quotes.text: Since age 15 or so, his main goal has been to build a self-improving Artificial Intelligence (AI) smarter than himself, then retire.
signature_quotes.context: Describing his lifelong ambition on his personal homepage.

signature_quotes.text: each new breakthrough comes twice as fast - Omega point around 2040
signature_quotes.context: Defining Schmidhuber's law regarding the accelerating pace of computer history.
